decisions.title: Ship Canvas V1 with Pass/Fail/Open Functions
decisions.context: Canvas execution status UI ready (DES-189), core functions implemented (ENG-9232), template components refactored (ENG-10073). Need decision on MVP scope vs. version history feature (DES-166) for initial release.

arguments.for: Version history can follow in V1.1; execution status + core functions = viable MVP aligned with 'Nail the Core' pillar. Gets Canvas into customer hands faster for feedback.
arguments.against: Version history is table stakes for collaborative decision tools. Shipping without it risks customer perception that Canvas is incomplete, delaying adoption.

decisions.title: Approve 100k Channel Limit Per Flow
decisions.context: Hard limit implemented (ENG-9051) to prevent scale issues. Async channel creation infrastructure ready (ENG-10315, ENG-8934). Need GTM approval before enforcing limit in production.

arguments.for: Protects platform stability; async creation handles scale gracefully. 100k channels = massive flows already. Aligns with 'Build the Moat' by ensuring reliability at scale.
arguments.against: Could surprise power users with large flows. Need customer communication plan and migration path for 5 affected customers identified in ENG-9097 RFC.

decisions.title: Resolve DLv2/CSRS Read-Path Dedup Divergence Before Gov Launch
decisions.context: Critical data consistency bug (ENG-10316): DLv2 and CSRS return different rows for identical dedup keys. Blocks FedRAMP lander (GTM-117) and gov deployment. Shadow traffic shows divergence (ENG-10282).
decisions.blockedBy: Root cause analysis in progress (ENG-10282). Worker query bypass (ENG-10257) may be related—investigation needed to confirm scope.

arguments.for: Data integrity is non-negotiable for gov customers. Fix now prevents future escalations and protects 'Embed Deeper' pillar by ensuring platform reliability.
arguments.against: Could delay FedRAMP launch by 2-3 weeks. Workaround: document known limitation and commit to fix in 30 days post-launch to meet government fiscal year deadline.

decisions.title: Prioritize Sift Edge Checkpoint Metrics Over Soak Test Update
decisions.context: Checkpoint metrics (ENG-10246) provide visibility into Edge runtime health. Soak test update (ENG-9773) validates v0.9.0 but is lower urgency. Both compete for Edge eng capacity.

arguments.for: Checkpoint metrics = operability signal for customers running Edge in production (Embed Deeper). Soak test can wait until next sprint without customer impact.
arguments.against: Soak test validates stability of v0.9.0 before wider rollout. Delaying risks undiscovered edge cases in production deployments.

decisions.title: Make Family Docs Publicly Indexed Now
decisions.context: Family docs published (ENG-9741) but not indexed (ENG-10313). Public indexing = SEO + discoverability for 'Ask SIFT' use cases. RFC findings (ENG-9097) suggest families are adoption blocker without clear docs.

arguments.for: Immediate SEO value; unblocks self-serve onboarding (Nail the Core). No engineering lift—just metadata change.
arguments.against: Docs may need quality review first. Publicly indexing incomplete docs could hurt brand perception.

timeAudit.weekSummary: 38% meeting load. 3 focus blocks protected. 2h unscheduled buffer.

Week trending toward 35+ meeting hours—consider declining 2 recurring syncs to protect <30h target
